comments.text: EP-734 reduces, conditional on the existence of a flat sloped $L$-line shifted-correlation code over an odd prime field, to a finite-field support-realization problem; the reduction is via diagonal lift plus a hole-histogram concentration lemma. The note also catalogs $13$ specific failure modes (hyperplane $\operatorname{Var}(K)=O(Q)$, Boolean-modulated Parseval, paired-cut separation-count, Latin-square obstructions, etc.) with structural reasons each family fails, and distills $9$ requirements R1--R9 any successful method must satisfy. Note

Potentially related OEIS: A031436 https://oeis.org/A031436 This is number of proper linear spaces of order n. This counts the same class of objects (non-trivial pairwise balanced designs), but I think is purely enumerative and does not capture the block-size distribution Erdős asked about. Please remove this comment if the adjacent entry is not deemed relevant enough for the problem, thanks.

Minor correction: the result of Erdős and de Bruijn also relies on the pairwise balanced block design being non-trivial. (Also, perhaps you might want to explicitly state what trivial means - I know that $m = 1, A_1 = \{1,...,n\}$ is a trivial case, but are there any others?) Feel free to delete this comment after addressing.
